informal: Let $A$ be a normal subgroup of a group $G$, and suppose that $b \in G$ is an element of prime order $p$, and that $b \not\in A$. Show that $A \cap (b) = (e)$.
lean:
theorem Herstein_exercise_2_10_1 {G : Type*} [Group G] (A : Subgroup G)
  [A.Normal] {b : G} (hb : b ∉ A) (hp : Nat.Prime (orderOf b)) :
  A ⊓ (Subgroup.closure {b}) = ⊥
reasoning: The Lean signature captures all components of the informal statement: A is a subgroup of an arbitrary group G with the [A.Normal] instance (matching "normal subgroup"); b ∉ A (hb) matches "b ∉ A"; Nat.Prime (orderOf b) matches "b has prime order p" (the prime p is just existentially encoded as the orderOf b, which is the standard formalization). The conclusion A ⊓ Subgroup.closure {b} = ⊥ is exactly "A ∩ ⟨b⟩ = {e}", since Subgroup.closure {b} is by definition the cyclic subgroup generated by b and ⊥ is the trivial subgroup. No extra assumptions (like finiteness of G) are silently introduced, and none are dropped. This is a logically equivalent formalization.
